description: A horizontal designed graphic in the DHS '#PetPreparedness' employee-feature template. Cream/beige background with a faint repeating pattern of dog-bone, fish, and paw-print line art. Top-left brown rounded banner with white serif text 'Pet Preparedness Tips.' Dark sans-serif body text reads: 'My 90lb 9-year-old Doberman, Helios, has quite the kit! For the day-to-day (walks and brewery trips) he has a folding bowl, a Nalgene for water, doggie-safe sunscreen, a cooling vest, a packable Ruffwear travel bed, his step-count tracking Fi collar, poop bags, and some snacks (this helps him do some training, helps him to behave when there are distractions, and helps get big smiles for photo ops!).' On the right is a rounded color selfie photo of a smiling woman in sunglasses and a brown T-shirt outdoors on grass next to a tan/fawn Doberman with its mouth open (looking like it's smiling). Below the photo, bold black serif text reads 'Ashley P. & Helios,' with a teal accent line, 'CISA' and 'Section Chief, Planning and Coordination.' The DHS seal is bottom-right. A humanizing public-affairs graphic featuring a CISA employee and her dog to promote pet summer-safety preparedness; target audience is the general public and pet owners.
notable_text: Pet Preparedness Tips; Ashley P. & Helios; CISA; Section Chief, Planning and Coordination
